note: Session isolation: task_board shows 139 agents across all concurrent sessions (lc-*, tc-*, mcp-*, ib-*). No way to filter by session_id or lane prefix. My 4 IB lanes are buried in noise. Finding my lanes requires scanning all 40 shown entries. Proposed: task_board(quick=true, filter="ib-*") or task_board(session_id="current").

Stale agent pollution: 21 stale agents from prior sessions clog the task board (lc-diag 2982s, lifecycle-te 3908s, etc.). These are "started" but never completed and I can't dismiss them. They make it hard to assess my actual fleet health. Proposed: stale agents should auto-dismiss after N minutes, or task_board should hide agents from sessions that aren't the current one.

Handoff body truncation: read(action="messages") cuts handoff JSON bodies mid-string with "..." truncation. I cannot read full findings, blockers, or next_steps from coordination messages. I must wait for the background task completion notification (which includes the full output) to get the complete data. This means I have incomplete information when deciding whether to advance a lane. Proposed: handoff bodies should not be truncated, or read should have a "full" mode that returns complete message bodies.

Duplicate task completions: ib-error-gaps architect task completed twice — once as the main architect handoff, once again with subagent results (root-cause-analyst, impact-assessor, etc.). The second completion looked like a new result but was actually the subagent fan-out report. This wastes context and causes confusion about whether the lane actually advanced. Proposed: subagent fan-out results should be recorded in session_journal, not re-delivered as a duplicate task completion.

Critic latency: ib-bootstrap-phases critic launched at 00:56 and by 01:12 (16 min) had not completed. Critics spawn subagents (coupling-auditor, convergence-checker, etc.) which adds latency. For a session with 4 lanes, the critic wave becomes a bottleneck — each critic fanning out 5+ subagents means 20+ concurrent subagents in the review wave. Proposed: critics should have a "fast" mode that doesn't fan out subagents, or the orchestrator should be able to set max critic concurrency.

Lane ID truncation in task_board: my lane "ib-bootstrap-phases" displays as "ib-bootstrap" (truncated at 12 chars). "ib-error-gaps" becomes "ib-error-gap". "ib-sidecar-boundary" becomes "ib-sidecar-b". "ib-plugin-mcp-observe" becomes "ib-plugin-mc". This makes it ambiguous which lane is which when scanning the fleet. Proposed: lane IDs should not be truncated, or the display should use a wider column.

No wave-level progress tracking: I advance lanes individually, but there's no way to know "all cartographers in my session are done" without manually cross-referencing task_board + messages. A lane lifecycle is: cartographer→architect→critic→surgeon→trial→journalist. I need to check each lane's current agent phase individually. Proposed: read(action="messages") or task_board should have a "session_summary" that shows per-lane status: lane_id, current_wave, current_agent, elapsed.

Cross-session message noise: read(action="messages") returns handoffs from other orchestrator sessions (tc-*, lc-*, mcp-* lanes) that I didn't spawn. Even with sender="architect" and recipient="general-man-agent" filters, I get architect handoffs from the typecheck session. I have to mentally filter "which of these are my IB lanes?" by checking lane_id. Proposed: messages should be filterable by lane_id prefix or session_id, e.g., read(lane_prefix="ib-").
